steps.title: Use the seed-spreading evidence
steps.content: The passage begins with the concern that introduced species can be harmful, then reports that common waxbills spread seeds of endangered native plants. The conclusion should reflect that beneficial role.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A discusses eating non-native plants, which the passage never mentions. Choice B adds negative effects that are not shown. Choice C invents an earlier period of plant damage. The remaining option follows from the waxbills' benefit. The answer is D.
